thesis: Leading cloud data platform experiencing challenging period with shares down 57% from highs. Q4 FY2026 showed strong fundamentals: 30% product revenue growth, $4.8B cash, first year of positive free cash flow. Transitioning from pure data warehouse to AI Data Cloud with Cortex AI suite gaining traction. However, intensifying competition from Databricks, Microsoft Fabric, and AI bubble concerns creating valuation pressure. RSI 16.43 indicates severely oversold conditions.

drivers: AI Data Cloud positioning captures generative AI workloads
Snowflake Intelligence and Cortex Code reach mainstream adoption
Margin expansion continues toward 20%+ operating margins

risks: Databricks continues taking share in AI workloads (65% growth vs 30%)
Microsoft Fabric bundles data warehouse with Office 365
Growth deceleration continues below 20% in FY2028
AI hype cycle ends before Snowflake captures value
Valuation compression to 5-6x revenue on growth slowdown

base_case.assumptions: Revenue grows 25-27% through FY2028 then decelerates to 20%; AI products contribute meaningfully but don't transform growth; Margins continue expanding toward 15-20% operating margin; Stock trades at 10-12x revenue (reasonable for 25% growth).

catalysts.event: Q1 FY2027 Earnings — Guidance update critical
Q2 FY2027 Earnings — AI traction visibility
AI Product Adoption — Cortex Code, Intelligence metrics

key_risks: Intensifying competition from Databricks (65% growth) and Microsoft Fabric
Consumption model creates revenue volatility during economic slowdowns
Heavy stock-based compensation (34% of revenue, improving but dilutive)
RSI 16.43 indicates oversold but may signal fundamental concerns
